description: Mexican drug cartel based in Tijuana, Baja California, founded in 1987 by the Arellano-Félix family. Once one of the largest and most violent criminal groups in Mexico, it has been weakened since the 2006 Sinaloa Cartel incursion and the fall of its leaders. Currently reduced to a few cells, it has aligned with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and battles rivals for trafficking influence.

country_links.evidence_quote: In the 1990s, the Tijuana Cartel decided to expand their market opportunities in Colombia, exchanging drugs for weapons.
country_links.context: The Tijuana Cartel expanded into Colombia, exchanging drugs for weapons and forming alliances with Colombian cartels.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Tijuana Cartel has infiltrated the Mexican law enforcement and judicial systems and is directly involved in street-level trafficking within the United States.
country_links.context: The Tijuana Cartel is directly involved in street-level trafficking within the United States, and has operations in California and Nevada.
